quantification.why: Some comparisons are computed (West is "roughly 35% below the leader" and top two "drive over 60% of revenue"), but most rely on bare rankings with raw dollar values ("Gadget led all products", "North topped regions", "trailed").

specificity.why: Every recommendation names specific products/regions with figures — "Promote Gadget and Widget bundles, since they together drive over 60% of revenue", "Investigate the West region's underperformance", and "cross-selling higher-priced items like Doohickey".

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations: revenue concentration ("over 60% of revenue" in two products), the regional gap ("roughly 35% below the leader"), and the two-product lead rather than just restating totals.

quantification.why: Some comparisons carry computed figures like West being "roughly 35% below the leader" and bundles driving "over 60% of revenue," but the product comparisons are mostly bare rankings with raw dollars ("Gadget led all products... ahead of Widget") lacking computed gaps.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names specific products, regions, and figures—"Promote Gadget and Widget bundles, since they together drive over 60% of revenue," "Investigate the West region's underperformance," and cross-selling "Doohickey."

insight_density.why: The prose surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations including revenue concentration ("over 60% of revenue"), the regional gap ("roughly 35% below the leader"), and a cross-sell opportunity for higher-priced items.

quantification.why: Some comparisons carry computed figures (West "roughly 35% below the leader", "over 60% of revenue") but the product section relies on bare rankings with raw dollars ("Gadget led", Doohickey and Sprocket "trailed") rather than computed gaps.

specificity.why: The Gadget/Widget bundle and Doohickey cross-sell recs are named and data-grounded, but "target it with focused campaigns" is generic advice lacking any specific figure or tactic.

insight_density.why: The prose surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations—the two-product concentration ("together drive over 60% of revenue") and the West region's ~35% gap—beyond restating table totals.
